response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with broad language exposure, but C++ is peripheral to his career rather than a core competency — it appears only in a 2014–2015 role at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IoT/MQTT experience, IEC 62443 secure development practices, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0). The role demands a seasoned C++ engineer with deep industrial/embedded systems grounding; this candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with those requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance and low-latency systems work (Sociomantic: async I/O, fibers, millions of HTTP req/s) shows relevant concurrency thinking
Strong event-driven and message-broker architecture experience (GISCollective platform, Sociomantic micro-services)
Proven engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, technical mentorship, and public workshops
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — absent from his skills list entirely, with only minor historical usage (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015)
Zero evidence of industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, or any of the required industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity practices that are core to this role
